rulings.Case Title: JAMES DUCLAIR VS. VANGUARD PROPERTIES INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 19, 2018, Line 1. DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to Colin Hoops based on lack of standing, no opposition having been filed. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for the remaining Plaintiffs to allege: (1) facts in support of each cause of action as to each Plaintiff with requisite specificity as is required for fraud-based causes of action; (2) any allegations made against Vanguard in its separate capacities (e.g. developer, seller's agent, buyer's agent) to be clearly and separately made; (3) factual allegations regarding delayed discovery by each Plaintiff as to each alleged latent defect to be clearly made. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 3H RENOVATION SERVICES, LLC ET AL VS. BSI FINANCIAL SERVICES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default; Declaration Of Olivier J. Labarre
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 19, 2018, Line 3. DEFENDANTS FAY SERVICING, LLC, ANH PHAM, BSI FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC., SKYGROUP REALTY, INC'S Motion To Set Aside Default. Transferred to Department 302 to be heard on October 3, 2018. Parties are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copies of their respective filings in support of and in opposition to the motion to Department 302 no later than September 20, 2018 with cover letters reflecting new hearing date and department. The Court is in receipt of courtesy copy of reply. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. VICENTE MANUEL JR ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 19, 2018, Line 6. DEFENDANT VICENTE MANUEL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Notice is fatally defective as it fails to state "the exact amount of rent claimed." Inclusion of two different amounts renders the Notice defective. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 4160-64 25TH STREET, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. GLEN STROUD ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 19, 2018, Line 7. DEFENDANT GLEN STROUD, PAUL MADGWICK'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. Notice of Termination is fatally defective as it failed to comply with SFRO 37.9A(e)(3)(C) and SFRO 37.9A(e)(4). Payment due under SFRO 37.9A(e)(3)(C) is determined based on the status of the tenant "at the time of the Notice of Intent to Withdraw." The Notice of Intent to Withdraw date, as pre-determined by Plaintiff and stated in the Notice of Termination (attached to the Complaint) is March 2, 2018. Per SFRO 37.9A(e)(3) and 37.9(f) the landlord has to notify the tenants of the right to receive payment/relocation assistance. The landlord failed to notify the tenant of the correct amount of the payment/assistance due under SFRO 37.9A(e)(3)(C). The landlord concedes that the amount of the relocation payment due is to be stated in the Notice of Termination (as opposed to the notice under SFRO 37.9A(f)(5)). (Opposition 8:3-11). The amount stated was incorrect (Defendant's RJN Ex. 2; RJN is granted). This ruling is limited to the facts of this case and language of the operative Notice of Termination. =(501/REQ)
